results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "The problem reduces to minimizing f(x1,x2) = (51/2)x1^2 + 13x1x2 + (19/2)x2^2 + 5x1 + x2 subject to x2 ≤ 4, x1+x2 ≥ 1.5, 3x1+x2 ≥ 4, with x3 = x1 from the equality constraint. The unconstrained minimum is infeasible. Analyzing the boundaries, the point where 3x1+x2 = 4 is active gives the KKT point x1 = 29/24 ≈ 1.2083, x2 = 3/8 = 0.375. Substituting yields the optimal value 50.875.", "answer": 50.875}

results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "The quadratic program is convex (P positive definite). Using the equality constraint A x = b, we substitute x3 = 7 - x2 to reduce the problem to two variables x1, x2. The objective becomes f(x1, x2) = 2.5x1^2 + 33x2^2 + 9x1x2 - 41x1 - 310x2 + 794.5. The constraints reduce to: -4x1 + 4x2 ≤ 11, x1 - 6x2 ≤ -16, x1 + x2 ≥ 2, 2x1 - x2 ≤ 2. The unconstrained minimum (at x1 = -28/83 ≈ -0.337, x2 = 1181/249 ≈ 4.742) violates the first constraint (x2 ≤ x1 + 2.75). Thus the optimum lies on the boundary -4x1 + 4x2 = 11, i.e., x2 = x1 + 11/4. Substituting this into the objective gives a 1D quadratic in x1: f(x1) = (89/2)x1^2 - (579/4)x1 + 3065/16. The minimizer is x1 = 579/356 ≈ 1.626, which lies within the feasible segment [-0.1, 4.75] and satisfies the other constraints. The corresponding optimal value is f_min = 210329/2848 ≈ 73.8515, which rounds to 73.85 within the required 0.01 tolerance.", "answer": 73.85}

results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "The equality constraint gives x1 = 4. Substituting x1 = 4 into the objective and constraints reduces the problem to minimizing f(x2, x3) = 8.5x2^2 + 20.5x3^2 - 7x2 x3 + 26x2 - 21x3 + 100 subject to -x2 + 3x3 <= 6, 4x2 - x3 <= 14, 3x2 + x3 <= 15, x3 <= x2. The unconstrained minimizer of f violates x3 <= x2, so the optimum lies on x3 = x2. Substituting gives f(t) = 22t^2 + 5t + 100 with t = x2 = x3. The unconstrained minimum along this line is at t = -5/44 ≈ -0.1136, which satisfies all other constraints. The optimal value is 8775/88 ≈ 99.7159, which rounds to 99.72.", "answer": 99.72}

results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "We minimize the quadratic objective (1/2)x^T P x + q^T x with P = [[18,3,-9],[3,5,-5],[-9,-5,9]], q = [0,4,0], subject to Gx <= h and Ax = b. Using the equality constraint 2x1 - 3x2 + x3 = -6, we eliminate x3 = -6 - 2x1 + 3x2. Substituting into the objective and inequalities gives a reduced problem in x1, x2. The unconstrained minimizer of the reduced objective violates the second inequality (8x1 - 11x2 <= -35), so we impose it as an equality. Solving the resulting KKT system yields x1 = 14053/16289, x2 = 62049/16289, x3 = 60307/16289, with Lagrange multiplier λ = 3036/1253 > 0. All other constraints are satisfied. The optimal objective value is computed exactly as 8,090,176,185 / 265,331,521 ≈ 30.4908, which rounds to 30.49.", "answer": 30.49}
